title: epipactis atropurpurea var. tremolsii
intro: Epipactis atropurpurea var. tremolsii, also known as the purple helleborine, is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid native to the Pyrenees mountains. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, where it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Epipactis atropurpurea var. tremolsii are thought to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions.
